name: June Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the chicken stock by boiling a whole chicken with a cut lemon inside for just over 1 hour, adding a peeled onion and carrot along with black peppercorns and sea salt to the water. Strain off the broth and refrigerate overnight.
2. Soak split peas overnight or for several hours, then boil in plenty of unsalted water for 30 minutes to 1 hour until soft.
3. Peel onions, turnips, and carrots; scrape fennel. Chop all vegetables into small dice.
4. In a heavy, covered pan, soften the diced vegetables with olive oil for 15 minutes.
5. Crush garlic with sea salt in a mortar, then add to the vegetables along with tomatoes and thyme or marjoram. Cook uncovered for 5-20 minutes, breaking up tomatoes with a wooden spoon.
6. Add drained split peas, skimmed stock, and washed sorrel leaves (spinach can be used). Bring to a boil, simmer for 30 minutes, then cool and serve. Adjust salt if needed; avoid liquidizing the soup unless you want the split pea flavor dominant.
